summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that Gogs 0.14.3 accepts multipart text fields without per-field size limits, causing significant memory consumption (40.4 MB RSS increase from a 10 MB text field). The server accepted the request with HTTP 400 but did not reject it for exceeding per-field size limits, confirming the CWE-770 vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: A multipart text field of 10 MB (no filename attribute) should be accepted and buffered entirely into memory, demonstrating that ParseMultipartForm only limits total body size, not individual field sizes.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the gogs/gogs:latest image, started Gogs 0.14.3 in Development mode, sent a 10 MB multipart text field via POST to /user/sign_up, and observed a 40.4 MB RSS increase (baseline 88336 kB to 129668 kB). The server responded with HTTP 400 but did not reject the request with HTTP 413 (per-field size limit), confirming no per-field size limit exists.
verification_method: The exploit measures process RSS memory before and after sending the large multipart text field. A 10 MB text field causing a 40.4 MB RSS increase confirms the field was buffered entirely into memory. The verify.sh script checks for a proof file written by exploit.py that records the memory measurements and server response.
evidence_produced_by_run: Baseline RSS: 88336 kB (measured by ps before exploit)
Post-exploit RSS: 129668 kB (measured by ps after sending 10 MB field)
RSS delta: 40.4 MB (4x amplification of the 10 MB input field)
Server response: HTTP 400 (not 413 - no per-field size limit enforcement)
Server remained responsive after the large request

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
